ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn authentication blocks compose forms and fields, while consumer-owned async flow switching, abort recovery, password-manager-safe native behavior, MFA/recovery paths, and opt-in rate-limit timing remain application decisions.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: The kit composes canonical native-form controls across sign-in, sign-up, password reset, passkey, MFA, and recovery-code paths with controlled or uncontrolled flow, abort-aware consumer submission, explicit ownership, and independently selectable recovery context.

visualSignature.summary: The authentication workbench uses literal white, Ink structure, a measured Green header seam, Violet focus, strong hierarchy, restrained corners, touch-aware targets, and preference fallbacks shared across Mergora.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A labelled navigation exposes only the access and recovery flows the consumer explicitly supplies.

api.enableWhen: Set showFlowNavigation to true and provide availableFlows.

disabledBehavior.ui: No flow navigation or additional flow labels are rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The selected controlled or uncontrolled native form remains usable.
disabledBehavior.events: No navigation activation or onFlowChange callback can occur.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No navigation landmark, current-page state, list, buttons, or descriptions enter the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A bounded countdown explains when another attempt becomes available and emits one readiness callback without owning the authoritative rate limit.

api.enableWhen: Set showRateLimitRecovery to true; a rate-limited result supplies the bounded duration.

disabledBehavior.ui: No countdown or recovery-ready output is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The authoritative error message remains visible and form behavior is unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No timer is installed and onRateLimitReady is never invoked.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No polite countdown output or timing update enters the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An optional aside makes the consumer-owned credential, session, authorization, and recovery boundary visible beside the form.

disabledBehavior.ui: No security-boundary aside or copy is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Authentication fields, validation, submit, and reset remain unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement events or callbacks exist.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No complementary context heading or prose enters the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit axe coverage includes basic and enhanced output, read-only reset cancellation, narrow RTL, reduced motion, forced colors, and target sizing; complete Risk Class 3 password-manager/passkey-device and manual assistive-technology evidence is not recorded.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit evidence covers enhancement removal, mutable and read-only native reset semantics, controlled and uncontrolled flow ownership, async result recovery, RTL, reduced motion, forced colors, and narrow/touch presentation; password-manager, passkey-device, lifecycle, and failure matrices remain open.

parityEvidence.summary: Package output is intentionally not planned by the catalog. Deterministic generation keeps canonical source, native registry, and Shadcn payloads on the same consumer transform, while the exact-tarball Next.js and Vite record verifies source mode and the source-only package boundary.

blockers.summary: Dependency closure, password-manager/passkey device matrices, security/privacy/legal review, current manual assistive technology, public dogfooding, and Passport approval remain open.

maturityAssessment.rationale: The source workflow and focused evidence exist, but distribution, lifecycle, browser/device, security, privacy, legal, and manual assistive-technology gates remain incomplete.
